free_text: Gamma-rays were detected with a shielded
Ge(Li) diode having efficiency at 1.33 MeV of 19%
relative to a 7.5 x 7.5 cm2 Nal crystal.
For the main series of automated measurements, the
targets were mounted at 90 degr. to the incident beam,
and the detector was located at 135 degr. with its
front face 155 mm from the sample.
Integral calculations were made to obtain the stopping
power correction factors necessary to derive pure
element yeilds from measurements of compound and
An aluminium reference sample was included in each
series of measurements. The variation from run to run
